---
title: ""
---

## Blog

- **21/05/2026.** Just saw this citation by Scott Peacor today on [Dynamic Ecology blog](https://dynamicecology.wordpress.com/2026/05/18/designing-a-statistic-project-to-show-that-we-ecologists-are-doing-something-wrong-is-not-my-idea-of-fun-interview-with-scott-peacor-on-meta-analysis-in-ecology/) today.

  ::: {}
  > I want my work to have a positive influence, not just have fun. This is not fun for me. I don’t really like statistics and I don’t like criticizing people. I don’t get a kick out of it. And so designing a statistic project to show that we ecologists are doing something wrong is not my idea of fun.
  :::

Found it striking (and inspirational)? Reminds me the [famous citation from Richard McElreath](https://elevanth.org/blog/2025/07/09/which-kind-of-science-reform/): 'Research is a job, not a hobby' (famous at least for me, haha).
